Done. Summary of the review of the Albanese CodimOneExtension + Thm32 blueprint slice.

## Verified

**Finding 2 (dead placeholder pins) — largely already resolved, one action taken.**
- Neither `lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly` nor `lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula` currently carries a `\lean{}` pin in the `.tex` (those were already stripped in session 0015), and neither `AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.stage6_regular_stalk_assembly` nor `...ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension` exists in `blueprint/lean_decls`. So the dead-pin issue at the pin level was already gone — nothing to remove from `lean_decls`.
- `lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly`: confirmed **orphaned** — `horizon leandag` shows 0 dependents, and no `\uses{...}` anywhere references it. Superseded by `thm:standard_smooth_regular_at_prime`. **Removed** the node (statement + proof, former lines 589-644).
- `lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula`: **NOT orphaned** — still `\uses`-linked from `lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field` (line 463), which is live through the closed-point regularity route (`...regular_at_rational_point` → reducedness lemmas → the Thm32 AV chain). Per your instruction I did **not** remove it (that would dangle `lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field`).

**Correspondence pass (session nodes) — all `\leanok` honest.**
- `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (the single Milne 3.3 sorry, `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): statement-level `\leanok` only; its proof is **not** `\leanok`. Correct.
- Its transitive consumers `lem:av_indeterminacy_empty` and `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends`: statement `\leanok`, proof **not** `\leanok`. Correct.
- `thm:codim_one_extension`, `cor:codim_one_free`, `thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2` (independent of the sorry): proof-level `\leanok` present. `\uses` edges on the new corollary and remark are correct.

## Edited (both files under my write scope)

`Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`:
- Removed the orphaned Stage 6.C assembly node.
- Rewrote the "superseded decomposition" subsection into a pure-math description of the Krull-height route and retitled it; **kept its `\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}`** because `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex:708` (out of my scope) `\cref`s it — deleting the label would break that reference.
- Rewrote the `subsec:codim1_substrate_lemmas` intro ("realised on the Lean side… regular-stalk assembly") and a second "realised on the Lean side" sentence (Stage 6.B intro) into pure math.
- Fixed the Stage-6 subsection intro that referred to the now-removed "(6.C)" lemma.
- Fixed a real LaTeX bug: a duplicate `\end{remark}` in `rmk:codim1_no_extension_from_codim1free` (remark envs now balanced 4/4).

`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`:
- Rewrote the rendered project-history clause "which the project excised at iter-163" and its `\texttt{sec:...}` label into a `\cref{chap:Jacobian}`.

## Final DAG
`horizon blueprint` + `horizon leandag`: Albanese 299→298 nodes, 163→160 edges, **0 dangling**, isolated count unchanged at 181 (no new orphans), 289 proved unchanged. All touched LaTeX environments balanced. Did not touch Lean files; did not run `lake build`.

## Could not / did not fix (reporting for your decision)
- The specific leak tokens I-0049 named (`b80f227`, `proved axiom-clean`, `removed as dead code`, `StandardSmoothDimension.lean`) were **already absent** — cleaned in a prior session.
- Non-rendered `%` archival comments remain project-history-laden: the top-of-file `STRATEGY NOTE` blocks (`iter-174`, `iter-163`, `iter-198`, `Plan-Agent`, "writer report") and `% SOURCE`/`% SOURCE QUOTE` blocks. These do not render in the blueprint PDF, so I left them; if you want a deeper strip of comment metadata, that's a larger pass worth a separate directive.
- The dedicated "Lean encoding" sections still reference target `.lean` files (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean`) in rendered prose — this is an established, deliberate encoding-guidance convention across these chapters (paired with the `% archon:covers` directive), not the "added narrative" the finding flagged, so I left it. Flag if you want those sections trimmed to pure math too.
